title: rubus arduennensis subsp. obtusatus
intro: Rubus arduennensis subsp. obtusatus is a subspecies of raspberry native to the Ardennes region of Europe, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is known for its high antioxidant content and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and inflammation. The medicinal properties of Rubus arduennensis subsp. obtusatus are attributed to its high levels of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its health benefits such as improving cardiovascular health, reducing cancer risk, and enhancing cognitive function. Rubus arduennensis subsp. obtusatus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to make jams, jellies, and other food products, making it a valuable resource for both culinary and medicinal purposes.
